diagrams.title: Basic Logic Gates Symbols
diagrams.description: Standard symbols for the three fundamental logic gates AND, OR, and NOT along with derived gates NAND, NOR, and XOR
elements: AND gate: D-shaped with flat input side, curved output side with bullet
OR gate: Shield shape with pointed output
NOT gate: Triangle with small circle at output point
NAND: AND with bubble on output
NOR: OR with bubble on output
diagrams.purpose: RECOGNIZE AND DRAW LOGIC GATE SYMBOLS IN CIRCUIT DIAGRAMS

diagrams.title: Digital Signal vs Analog Signal
diagrams.description: Comparison showing continuous analog waveform versus discrete digital square wave
elements: Analog: Smooth sine wave varying continuously
Digital: Square wave with only two levels (high=1, low=0)
Shows voltage levels marked as Vmax (logic 1) and Vmin (logic 0)
diagrams.purpose: UNDERSTAND DIFFERENCE BETWEEN CONTINUOUS AND DISCRETE SIGNAL REPRESENTATION

diagrams.title: 2-to-1 Multiplexer Block Diagram
diagrams.description: Internal logic implementation of a 2-to-1 multiplexer using basic gates
elements: Two data inputs: I0 and I1
Implementation: S'·I0 + S·I1 using AND-OR gates
Truth table showing select line control
diagrams.purpose: UNDERSTAND HOW MULTIPLEXERS WORK AND CAN BE IMPLEMENTED

diagrams.title: 4-Variable Karnaugh Map Layout
diagrams.description: Standard arrangement of cells in a 4-variable K-map showing Gray code ordering
elements: Variables A,B on rows (00,01,11,10 Gray code)
Variables C,D on columns (00,01,11,10 Gray code)
Adjacent cells horizontally and vertically differ by only one variable
diagrams.purpose: USE K-MAP FOR BOOLEAN FUNCTION SIMPLIFICATION
